worth_it_explanation: DV Authentic Attention scores ad exposures on presentation quality and interaction signals that a binary viewable/not-viewable call does not capture. Without acting on those scores, an advertiser pays the same CPM for an ad that technically met the MRC viewability threshold but was scrolled past in under a second and one that was on-screen for five seconds in a readable position. These checks confirm that attention measurement is configured with a goal rather than left as a reporting add-on, and that attention signal data is used to adjust buying rather than sit in a dashboard.
intro: DV Authentic Attention is DoubleVerify's proprietary attention measurement product, available through DV Pinnacle. It scores each ad impression on two composite dimensions: Exposure Quality (covering factors such as screen real estate, time in view, and audibility) and Interaction Quality (covering signals such as mouse movement and scroll behavior near the ad). The score supplements, rather than replaces, the Media Rating Council (MRC) viewability standard. An impression can be MRC-viewable and score low on attention, or it can score high on attention and be genuinely engaged-with inventory. These checks audit whether attention measurement is integrated into the buying workflow or treated as a passive reporting layer.

audit_checks.title: Campaign optimizes on viewability alone and does not use attention signals to distinguish high-quality from low-quality viewable supply
audit_checks.impact_rationale: An MRC-viewable impression covers a wide spectrum of actual exposure quality. An ad that meets the 50 percent on-screen for one-second threshold in a cluttered position at the bottom of a long page scores the same as one that rendered full-screen above the fold for five seconds. Without attention scoring, the campaign cannot distinguish between the two and may allocate budget toward technically compliant but low-engagement inventory.

educate.base:
The MRC viewability standard, introduced in 2014, defines a display ad as viewable when at least 50 percent of its pixels are on screen for at least one continuous second. For video, the threshold is 50 percent of pixels on screen for at least two continuous seconds. These thresholds were designed as a minimum floor to exclude ads that never had a chance to be seen, not as a proxy for whether an ad actually received attention.

DV Authentic Attention adds a layer of measurement above that floor. It evaluates each impression on Exposure Quality (how much of the ad was visible, for how long, at what size, and with what audio conditions) and Interaction Quality (whether user behavior near the ad suggested active engagement). The result is a composite attention score attached to each impression. Impressions that meet the MRC threshold but score low on attention represent technically compliant placements that may produce little advertising value.

When a campaign optimizes exclusively on viewability, the demand-side platform (DSP) bids toward any impression that passes the viewability threshold. Two placements with identical viewability rates can have substantially different attention profiles depending on placement position, page layout, content adjacency, and user behavior. The campaign has no way to favor the higher-attention placement without the score data feeding the optimization.

Attention is a newer measurement concept with no single industry-standard definition. DV Authentic Attention uses DoubleVerify's own methodology; other vendors measure attention differently, and cross-vendor scores are not directly comparable. It is a supplementary signal rather than a replacement for viewability, and it should be evaluated as one input among several rather than a single source of truth.

A few signs this check applies:
  • DV Authentic Attention is enabled in DV Pinnacle and appears in reporting, but no attention-based goal or target is set on the campaign or line item in the DSP
  • The campaign's primary key performance indicator (KPI) is viewability rate (e.g., MRC viewable impressions divided by total impressions) with no secondary attention metric tracked alongside it
  • Post-campaign reporting from DV Pinnacle shows the attention score distribution but the data has not been used to adjust targeting, bidding, or supply-path decisions
You may want to confirm whether attention score data from DV Pinnacle is currently flowing into the DSP as a signal, and whether any campaign goal or private marketplace (PMP) filtering is applied to favor higher-attention inventory.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer running display prospecting campaigns, viewability-only optimization may concentrate budget on inventory that meets the MRC floor but produces low recall. You may want to confirm whether attention score data from DV Pinnacle is used to filter or prioritize supply selection, particularly for upper-funnel display formats where time-in-view affects brand recall.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser running awareness display before a retargeting phase, the quality of first-touch exposure affects how responsive that audience will be downstream. You may want to confirm whether attention scoring is informing which placements qualify for the prospecting campaign so that the retargeting pool reflects genuinely exposed users rather than technically viewable ones.

educate.learn_more_note: DoubleVerify's documentation for DV Authentic Attention, including the Exposure Quality and Interaction Quality components and how to set attention goals in DV Pinnacle, is available within the platform's help center, which requires a DV Pinnacle login to access. Your DV account team can walk through the specific configuration options for your account.

investigate.intro: Review the DV Pinnacle account settings to confirm whether DV Authentic Attention is active, and check the DSP to determine whether any attention-based goal, bid signal, or supply filter is in place.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and navigate to the account or campaign you are auditing.
Open the Measurement settings and confirm whether DV Authentic Attention is enabled for this account. If it is not enabled, this check does not apply further.

If attention is enabled, open the Analytics or Reporting section and pull an impression-level or aggregate report that includes the attention score columns (Exposure Quality, Interaction Quality, and the composite Authentic Attention score).

Review the score distribution. Note the percentage of impressions that fall in the lowest attention tier (the specific tier labels and thresholds are defined in DV Pinnacle) relative to the total measured impressions.

Confirm whether any attention-based goal, minimum threshold, or supply filter is currently active on the account or campaign. This may appear as an 'Attention Goal' setting, a PMP filter, or a bid factor in the connected DSP.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner confirms whether DV Authentic Attention is enabled and records whether any attention-based goal or supply filter is active. A campaign with attention measurement enabled and an attention goal or supply filter applied passes. A campaign with attention measurement enabled but no goal, filter, or DSP integration acting on the data is a fail, recorded with the campaign name and the attention score distribution observed in DV Pinnacle.
evaluate.fallback_message: Confirm whether DV Authentic Attention is enabled and record whether any attention-based goal or supply filter is active in DV Pinnacle or the connected DSP above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s campaigns optimize on MRC viewability alone and do not use DV Authentic Attention scores to distinguish high-quality viewable supply from low-quality viewable supply. Attention measurement is enabled in DV Pinnacle but the data is not feeding an attention goal, bid signal, or supply filter in the connected DSP, so technically viewable but low-engagement placements receive the same consideration as higher-attention inventory. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the attention score distribution in DV Pinnacle, setting an attention goal or minimum threshold on the account or campaign, and working with the DV account team to confirm how the attention signal can be passed to the DSP as a bid factor or supply-path filter.

audit_checks.title: Attention data is collected but has not resulted in a change to supply selection, creative rotation, or bidding
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Measuring attention without acting on the data produces measurement spend with no corresponding change in buying behavior. The account can report on attention scores, but if those scores have never influenced which supply is prioritized, which creative formats are rotated, or how much is bid for a placement, the measurement has not improved campaign outcomes.

educate.base:
DV Authentic Attention produces a per-impression score, and DV Pinnacle surfaces that score in aggregate reporting: by publisher, placement, creative size, device type, and environment. An account that has been running with attention measurement for one or more campaigns has a data set that can answer several specific questions: which publishers deliver consistently high-attention impressions, which creative formats score higher on Interaction Quality, and which device or environment combinations produce the best Exposure Quality scores.

If that data has been reviewed and no action has followed, the measurement has not yet produced a return on the cost of the DV Pinnacle integration. The most direct uses of attention data include excluding or deprioritizing consistently low-attention publishers from open exchange buying, negotiating PMPs with publishers whose attention scores are above a threshold, rotating to creative formats that the data shows produce higher attention (e.g., larger display units or full-screen video placements), and passing the attention score to the DSP as a bid factor so the algorithm prices attention-qualified inventory higher.

Attention scoring is a supplementary signal, not a directive. Some low-attention placements may still be cost-effective at a lower CPM; the goal is to make the trade-off visible and deliberate rather than invisible. An account that has reviewed the data and made a considered decision not to act on it is in a different position from one that has not reviewed the data at all. Both conditions show the same symptom in reporting, but the recommended next step differs.

A few signs this check applies:
  • DV Pinnacle shows multiple flights of attention data across publishers or formats, but no publisher has been excluded, deprioritized, or promoted based on attention score in any DSP or in a PMP list
  • The attention score distribution has not been shared with the agency or client as part of post-campaign analysis, and no action items from that analysis appear in the next campaign brief
  • Creative rotation decisions in the DSP are based on click-through rate or video completion rate, with no reference to which format produced higher attention scores in prior flights
You may want to confirm whether the attention data in DV Pinnacle has been reviewed alongside supply and creative decisions, and whether any finding from that review informed the current campaign setup.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer, attention data may indicate that certain content categories, device types, or placements consistently score low. You may want to confirm whether that insight has been used to update the exclusion list, adjust PMP terms, or rotate creative formats in the current campaign.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, attention score by publisher or format can inform which placements to favor in the upper-funnel phase before retargeting. You may want to confirm whether prior flight data has been used to adjust supply selection or creative format decisions in the current campaign brief.

educate.learn_more_note: DoubleVerify's documentation for reviewing attention analytics and applying attention insights to supply and creative decisions is available within the DV Pinnacle help center, which requires a DV Pinnacle login to access. Your DV account team can walk through the reporting views and actioning options for your account.

investigate.intro: Review DV Pinnacle's attention analytics and confirm whether the data has been used to adjust supply selection, creative rotation, or bidding in the DSP.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and navigate to the Analytics or Reporting section for the account or campaign.

Pull a report broken out by publisher or domain, filtered to impressions with attention scoring. Sort by composite attention score or by Exposure Quality to identify which publishers rank in the lowest tier.

Note the date range covered. If multiple flights are included, check whether the publisher list in the DSP or in any active PMP includes or excludes publishers based on their attention performance from prior flights.

Pull a report broken out by creative size or ad format. Note whether any format shows consistently higher or lower attention scores, and confirm whether those findings were used to inform creative rotation or format selection in the current campaign.

Ask the team whether attention score data from DV Pinnacle has been shared in a post-campaign report or optimization review, and whether any action items from that review were carried into the current campaign setup.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner reviews the attention data in DV Pinnacle and confirms whether any supply, creative, or bidding decision in the current or most recent campaign reflects an action taken in response to prior attention score data. An account with documented evidence that attention data informed at least one supply, creative, or bidding change passes. An account with attention data available across multiple flights but no evidence of any corresponding action is a fail, recorded with the number of flights measured and the observation that no action has been documented.
evaluate.fallback_message: Review the attention analytics in DV Pinnacle and record whether any supply, creative, or bidding decision in the current campaign reflects a finding from prior attention data above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s DV Authentic Attention measurement produces data that has not yet been used to adjust supply selection, creative rotation, or bidding. Attention scores are available in DV Pinnacle across one or more campaign flights, but there is no evidence that those scores have informed which publishers are included in open exchange buying, which formats are prioritized, or how the DSP prices attention-qualified inventory. {{voice_recommend}} scheduling a review of the attention analytics in DV Pinnacle with the DV account team, identifying the lowest-scoring publishers and formats, and documenting at least one supply or creative action to take into the next campaign brief so the measurement spend produces a corresponding change in buying behavior.
